level_signals: 12+ years of experience across strategy, operations, or general management roles
Experience setting direction, guiding execution, and driving accountability across teams
Lead and set the strategy for Whatnot's Operations vendor framework, deploying managers and specialists

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You should have 12+ years of experience across strategy, operations, or general management roles (e.g., consulting, operations, finance, GM)
Proven experience operating in high-growth scaling environments, with a strong track record of driving operational improvement and building and leading high performing teams
Strong strategic and analytical judgment, with the ability to synthesize data, metrics, and qualitative insights into clear recommendations and decisions
Experience leading large, cross-functional initiatives and managing complex, multi-threaded projects end to end
Proven ability to work with external partners or vendors in scaled, global operating models
Exceptional leadership skills, with experience setting direction, guiding execution, and driving accountability across teams
Ability to manage shifting priorities while maintaining structure, focus, and operational rigor
Proficiency in data analysis and reporting tools; experience with SQL and analytics platforms (e.g., Looker, Sigma, Hex)

scope.surface: Post-purchase operations including Customer Experience and Trust & Risk vendor operations; Service Delivery
scope.business_model: marketplace / livestream commerce platform, B2C and B2B sellers
core_responsibilities: Develop and execute commerce and operations strategies that support Whatnot's growth across post-purchase operations
Apply strong analytical rigor to operational and commerce data, surfacing insights that inform initiatives to improve quality, speed, and scalability across the Operations team
Lead and set the strategy for Whatnot's Operations vendor framework, deploying managers and specialists to drive outcomes, set priorities, guide execution, and ensure high-quality delivery
Define and track vendor KPIs, monitor quality and SLAs, and drive continuous performance improvement across partner sites
Lead strategic sourcing efforts to expand vendor coverage across new capabilities or geographies
Build scalable systems and processes that allow Customer Experience and Trust & Risk support teams to expand quickly
Partner cross-functionally with Product, Engineering, and other stakeholders to translate insights from user interactions into initiatives

green_flags: Forbes #1 Best Startup Employer in America recognition cited
Clear role scope with specific operational domains named (Customer Experience, Trust & Risk)
Strong benefits package including 16 weeks paid parental leave, 401k match, wellness and childcare allowances
Remote co-located model with flexibility across multiple US hubs
EOE statement with explicit diversity values
red_flags: Role requires being within commuting distance of NY, SF, Phoenix, or LA — not fully remote despite 'remote co-located' company description
12+ years experience threshold is high and may narrow the candidate pool significantly
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned anywhere in JD

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Director' and 12+ years requirement)
seniority.manages_people (inferred from 'deploying managers and specialists' and 'leading high performing teams')
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated as metrics)
compensation.equity_mentioned (no mention found; defaulted to false)
ambiguities: Company describes itself as a 'remote co-located team' but the role body states 'Team members in this role are required to be within commuting distance of our New York, San Francisco, Phoenix, or Los Angeles hubs' — this is hybrid/onsite-required, not remote. Treated as hybrid here.
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD; set to 'unclear'.
